final_message: Hello there! I am Pal, your friendly math tutor, and I am so happy to see you today! 🌟 Today we are going to learn about something really fun called reflection symmetry. Imagine you have a shape on a piece of paper. A line of symmetry is a special fold line that divides the shape into two halves that are perfect mirror images of each other. Some shapes have a vertical line of symmetry, which is a left-right mirror. Some have a horizontal line, which is a top-bottom mirror, and some even have both! You can use this neat idea to make shapes by drawing just half on folded paper, then cutting and unfolding it. For example, the letter A has a vertical line of symmetry right down the middle. Are you ready to explore some magical mirror shapes together?

orchestrator_intent.goal: Welcome the student and resume the lesson on reflection symmetry.
orchestrator_intent.reasoning: The session has just started. The dialogue agent should greet the student and continue teaching the current topic.

current_section_title: Reflection Symmetry and Lines of Symmetry
current_section_content: A line of symmetry is a fold line that divides a shape into two halves that are perfect mirror images of each other. Some shapes have a vertical line of symmetry (left-right mirror), some have a horizontal line (top-bottom mirror), and some have both. You can use this idea to cut out shapes by drawing only half or one-quarter of the shape on folded paper, then cutting and unfolding. Letters like A have one vertical line of symmetry, while letters like H have both vertical and horizontal lines of symmetry.
